title: Source ledger tracks provenance
canonical_summary: The source ledger records where claims come from so Atlas can distinguish evidence from memory or drafts.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain provenance as the backbone of source-governed RAG.
exactness_guard: Do not treat unsourced cards as verified.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Evidence grade matters
canonical_summary: Sources need priority so canonical/current evidence outranks weaker notes or archives.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain trust levels and why chunks are not equal.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Source coverage supports lanes
canonical_summary: Each lane needs source coverage that identifies what evidence supports its cards and claims.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain how coverage protects against hallucination.
exactness_guard: Do not call a lane validated without source coverage.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Archives are lower-priority memory
canonical_summary: Archived notes can preserve history but should not override current canonical or official sources.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
exactness_guard: Do not delete old evidence just because it is lower priority.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Claim has no source trail
canonical_summary: A claim without a source trail cannot be treated as verified Atlas evidence.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Flag as unsupported and queue for sourcing.
exactness_guard: Do not polish unsupported claims into stronger language.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Old archive overrides current guidance
canonical_summary: If old archives conflict with current guidance, source-priority rules should prevent stale override.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Name conflict and choose higher-priority source.
exactness_guard: Do not treat old notes as equal to current docs.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Source path is too broad
canonical_summary: A source reference that only says docs or notes is too broad for reliable source-governed retrieval.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Replace broad references with exact file/source paths later.
exactness_guard: Do not leave broad labels in production cards.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Unsupported vs low-confidence claim
canonical_summary: Unsupported claims have no evidence; low-confidence claims have weak, old, conflicting, or incomplete evidence.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
exactness_guard: Do not label unsourced claims as merely low confidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Archive source vs current source
canonical_summary: Use archives for history or idea recovery; use current sources for live instructions and volatile claims.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Branch by historical/stable vs current/volatile claim.
exactness_guard: Do not discard useful historical context.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Source ledger authority order
canonical_summary: Root guidance, validation rules, canonical lane files, official/current docs, and measured outputs outrank raw archives and memory.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: State source order and apply it consistently.
exactness_guard: Do not let memory override ledger evidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: Citations are not source governance
canonical_summary: A citation shows origin; source governance decides whether that source is authoritative enough for the claim.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
exactness_guard: Do not assume cited means correct or current.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.

title: How to answer source-ledger questions
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the claim, source status, priority, confidence, and action.
source_priority_rule: Use current project governance files, lane validation docs, source coverage, and QC/evaluation outputs before memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Use claim, source trail, priority, confidence, action.
exactness_guard: Do not strengthen a claim without evidence.
notes: Seed card for Atlas Reader Mini contract.
